headline: Sarawak Log Export Ban Exemptions Cover 62% of Timber Volume
context: Despite Sarawak's log export ban enacted in 2019, exemptions granted to 23 companies cover an estimated 62% of total timber volume, effectively nullifying the policy.

cards.big: Sarawak banned log exports in 2019 to promote domestic processing.
cards.sub: Exemptions to 23 companies now cover 62% of timber volume. The ban bans 38%.

cards.big: Exempted companies include 4 firms linked to the state chief minister's family.
cards.sub: Conflict of interest disclosures were not filed for any of the 23 exemption approvals.

cards.big: Domestic sawmills operate at 54% capacity due to raw log shortages.
cards.sub: Logs that could be processed locally are exported under exemption, costing 2,400 processing jobs.

cards.big: Indonesia's log export ban — without exemptions — created 1.2 million processing jobs.
cards.sub: Sarawak's exemption-riddled version has created none.

cards.big: A ban with 62% exemptions is not a ban. It is selective access disguised as policy.
Log export bans work when they are total. Sarawak's exemption system undermines both environmental and economic objectives. The policy needs to be a ban, not a permissions system.
